route.name: Caldera 500
route.description: Public GPX long Northern California volcanic backcountry route with forest roads, dirt, remote services, and fire-season exposure.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX long Northern California volcanic backcountry route with forest roads, dirt, remote services, and fire-season exposure.
route.bestSeason: Summer/fall only after snow, but wildfire/smoke and route length block the July weekend.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX long Northern California volcanic backcountry route with forest roads, dirt, remote services, and fire-season exposure.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water sources/gaps require route-by-route measurement and current checks.
resources.food: Sparse resupply; exact service sequence required.
resources.camping: Multi-night camp sequence must be planned separately.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Keep as a future California epic.
Do not promote full route for July 10-13.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Full route is 480 miles with about 60,000 ft climbing.
Summer heat, high-country closures, bear-storage rules, and water carries need segment-level planning.
Full Caldera 500 is rejected for July 10-13 due to 480 miles / 15 days / high difficulty.
Potential shorter segments remain segment leads only; no selected public-GPX segment has cleared Inyo closures, camp legality, bear/storage, water, and final weather/smoke.
requiredFixes: Do not promote the full route for this weekend.
Choose a short Mammoth/Bishop/Lower Rock Creek style segment and vet independently.
Choose and name a specific shorter public-GPX segment before further vetting.
Overlay selected segment against Inyo active closure orders and any other land managers it touches.
Confirm legal camps/lodging, bear-storage requirements, water carries, and resupply.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?
